notes: Verified via WebFetch directly against the official Palestinian National Information Center (info.wafa.ps/pages/details/29601), which explicitly lists '7 كانون الثاني' (7 January, Eastern/Orthodox Christmas) as a separate 1-day official paid holiday, distinct from the already-live christmas-palestine (25 December, Western Christmas).
Legal basis: Palestinian Labor Law No. 7 of 2000 — same legal basis already used for christmas-palestine.

Strictly civic/legal register (holiday-status facts only) — deliberately avoided any political content given Palestine's sensitivity; this is purely about which day is a paid holiday for Christian employees, not a political topic.

Explicitly addresses a real point of confusion: many readers assume 7 Jan is just an alternate name for 25 Dec Christmas rather than a genuinely separate paid holiday — this is the core differentiation angle.
2000 (law year) appears in content but is well below the hardcoded-year threshold (current year - 1 = 2025), no validator issue.
Single-country event (_countryCode: ps), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage.
